brief_justification: Paper A is semiclassical spectral theory: it builds a pseudodifferential calculus and uses complex scaling to prove a Weyl law counting quasinormal-mode resonances of a Schwarzschild black hole near threshold energy. Paper B is a compartmental epidemiological model adding an exposure state and a discrete age-of-infection/viral-load structure to describe infection probability. There is no role-for-role correspondence: resonance-counting for a Schrodinger-type operator and disease-spread dynamics are governed by entirely different machinery, so no coherent mapping can be filled in.
